evals.name: new-js-rest-api-load-test
evals.prompt: I need to write a k6 load test for our login API in JavaScript. The endpoint is POST /api/v1/auth/login with a JSON body of username and password. After login I need to call GET /api/v1/profile using the returned token. We expect 100 concurrent users at peak and our SLA is p95 under 800ms. I have a CSV file with test users.
evals.expected_output: A complete runnable JavaScript k6 script with: options block (ramping-vus to 100, thresholds p(95)<800), SharedArray loading the CSV file (parsed manually — no header row, split by comma), per-VU login inside default() using credentials from SharedArray (not a shared token from setup), groups and sleep, checks AND thresholds. Run command with --env BASE_URL.

assertions.name: uses_5_block_pattern
assertions.description: Script has options (scenarios+thresholds), SharedArray for CSV, default function with groups/checks/sleep — complete runnable structure

assertions.name: uses_ramping_vus_executor
assertions.description: Executor is ramping-vus with stages ramping up to 100 VUs

assertions.name: has_threshold_p95_800
assertions.description: Thresholds include p(95)<800 matching the stated SLA

assertions.name: per_vu_auth_not_shared_token
assertions.description: Each VU authenticates with its own credentials from SharedArray inside default(), not a single shared token from setup()

assertions.name: has_sleep_between_requests
assertions.description: default function includes at least one sleep() call between requests

assertions.name: uses_shared_array_for_csv
assertions.description: CSV user data is loaded via SharedArray at init context, not via open() or inline array inside default function

assertions.name: includes_run_command
assertions.description: Response includes the k6 run command with --env BASE_URL parameter

assertions.name: checks_plus_thresholds
assertions.description: Script uses both check() for per-request diagnostics AND thresholds for test pass/fail — not check() alone

evals.name: fix-checks-not-failing-test
evals.expected_output: Explains that check() records statistics but never fails the test — exit code 0 is always returned unless thresholds are defined. Delivers corrected script with export const options containing thresholds (http_req_failed rate<0.01 and checks rate>0.99). Explains the three failure mechanisms: check(), threshold (exit 99), and fail().

assertions.name: identifies_root_cause
assertions.description: Explains that check() never stops or fails the test — it only records pass/fail statistics, always exits 0

assertions.name: adds_thresholds
assertions.description: Fixed script adds export const options with thresholds block — at minimum http_req_failed or checks rate threshold

assertions.name: keeps_check_for_diagnostics
assertions.description: check() is kept in the fixed script alongside thresholds — not removed

assertions.name: explains_three_mechanisms
assertions.description: Explains check() vs threshold (exit 99) vs fail() and when to use each

evals.name: wrong-executor-for-rps-target
evals.prompt: I need my k6 test to maintain exactly 200 requests per second for 10 minutes to validate our API's throughput capacity. I'm using ramping-vus but the RPS keeps varying depending on response time. What's the right way to do this?
evals.expected_output: Explains open vs closed workload model. Delivers script using constant-arrival-rate with rate:200, timeUnit:'1s', duration:'10m', preAllocatedVUs calculated as ceil(200 × p95_seconds × 1.2), maxVUs as safety ceiling. Warns about dropped_iterations metric.

assertions.name: explains_open_vs_closed
assertions.description: Explains why ramping-vus (closed model) varies throughput with response time, and why arrival-rate (open model) maintains fixed RPS

assertions.name: uses_constant_arrival_rate
assertions.description: Fixed script uses constant-arrival-rate executor

assertions.name: sets_rate_200
assertions.description: rate is set to 200 and timeUnit to '1s'

assertions.name: includes_preAllocatedVUs_with_formula
assertions.description: preAllocatedVUs is set with an explanation of the formula: rate × p95_seconds × 1.2

assertions.name: includes_maxVUs
assertions.description: maxVUs is set as a hard ceiling above preAllocatedVUs

assertions.name: mentions_dropped_iterations
assertions.description: Warns that iterations are silently dropped when VUs run out, and explains how to detect via dropped_iterations metric

evals.name: memory-issue-large-data-file
evals.expected_output: Identifies root cause: a plain variable in init context creates one copy of the parsed JS object per VU — for 200 VUs this multiplies memory by 200×. Fix is SharedArray — data is loaded once and shared as read-only across all VUs. Explains that open() in init context is correct; the problem is the plain variable, not open() itself. Delivers corrected script with SharedArray.

assertions.name: identifies_correct_root_cause
assertions.description: Explains that the plain variable creates a per-VU copy of the parsed data — memory usage = data_size × VU_count. Does NOT say open() causes OOM on its own.

assertions.name: uses_shared_array
assertions.description: Fixed script uses SharedArray at init context (top level)

assertions.name: explains_shared_array_benefit
assertions.description: Explains that SharedArray holds one copy of the data shared across all VUs — memory does not scale with VU count

assertions.name: clarifies_open_is_init_only
assertions.description: Clarifies that open() is an init-context-only function — calling it inside default() causes a runtime error, not OOM

evals.name: websocket-load-test
evals.prompt: I need to load test a WebSocket chat API. The flow is: connect to wss://chat.example.com/ws with a JWT token in the Authorization header, send a JSON message every 5 seconds, validate that the server echoes back within 200ms, and disconnect after 30 seconds. We need to simulate 50 concurrent connections.
evals.expected_output: Complete k6 script importing from k6/ws. Uses constant-vus with 50 VUs and duration covering the 30s session. ws.connect() params include Authorization header. socket.setInterval() sends a message with timestamp every 5s. socket.on('message') checks roundtrip latency < 200ms. socket.on('error') registered. socket.setTimeout() closes after 30s. check on ws.connect() response status 101.

assertions.name: imports_k6_ws
assertions.description: Script uses 'import ws from k6/ws'

assertions.name: uses_constant_vus_50
assertions.description: Options use constant-vus executor with 50 VUs

assertions.name: auth_header_in_connection
assertions.description: ws.connect() params object includes headers with Authorization

assertions.name: set_interval_every_5s
assertions.description: socket.setInterval() is used to send messages with a 5000ms (5s) interval

assertions.name: roundtrip_latency_check
assertions.description: socket.on('message') handler checks that elapsed time since send is < 200ms

assertions.name: error_handler_registered
assertions.description: socket.on('error') is registered to catch connection failures

assertions.name: timeout_closes_connection
assertions.description: socket.setTimeout() closes the connection after ~30 seconds as a safety net
